attributes.description: Alerts when service error rate exceeds 5%. (Rule is a generic placeholder that should be renamed for NGINX.)
attributes.name: High error rate by service

investigation_guide.blob:
## NGINX: generic error-rate rule (placeholder)

### What fired
`error_rate_pct > 5%` on a service in the log-based query.

### Why it matters
This rule appears to be a leftover myservice template and should be reworked as a real NGINX rule. Treat any alert from it with skepticism until it is corrected.

### Triage
1. Inspect the rule query — confirm it targets NGINX datasets.
2. Consider disabling until corrected.

### Remediation
- Replace the rule query with an NGINX-specific one (e.g. 5xx error rate).

### Tuning
- Placeholder — should be retired or reworked.
